rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY PONTINO VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 12, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Crane Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 through 4 (negligence and strict liability) and off calendar as to issues 5 and 6 (false representation and punitive damages). Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to an asbestos-containing product or material attributable to defendant (the asbestos content issue). Defendant also failed to sustain its burden of production of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that any exposure to asbestos attributable to defendant was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to his asbestos-related disease. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and responses to defendant¿s written contention discovery preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. The court did not consider the evidence submitted with defendant¿s reply. Plaintiff represented that he has dismissed his false representation cause of action and claim for punitive damages. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: MATTIE MCFALL et al VS. ARVIN MERITOR, INC et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 12, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Hennessy Industries, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden of production and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant¿s product(s) at issue contained asbestos. Plaintiffs also failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether liability may be imposed on defendant regarding exposure to asbestos from the use of defendant¿s product(s) in conjunction with asbestos-containing brake materials. Plaintiffs¿ objections to Mr. Mountz¿s declaration are overruled. A product manufacturer has no duty to persons whose injury results from defects in the products of others. (Garman v. Magic Chef, Inc. (1981) 117 Cal.App.3d 634; Taylor v. Elliott Turbomachinery Co., Inc. 171 (2009) Cal.App.4th 564). Tellez-Cordova v. Campbell-Hausfeld/Scott Fetzger Co. (2004) 129 Cal.App.4th 577 is distinguishable because the allegations there were that the wheels and discs were harmless without the power supplied by respondents¿ tools, while here the allegations are that Hennessy¿s machines were used on asbestos-containing brakes. It is the asbestos content of a product that makes it inherently dangerous. (Taylor v. Elliott Turbomachinery Co. (2009) 171 Cal.App.4th 564.) Defendant¿s machines are more analogous to the stove in Garman. As in that case, it was a defect in the product of another which proximately caused the injury. The only alleged defect in Hennessy¿s machines is that they caused another defective product to injure the decedent. The defendant has no duty under these facts. Plaintiffs¿ request for sanctions is denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: OSCAR BONETTO, ET AL VS. DOWMAN PRODUCTS, INC.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 12, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Westside Building Material Corporation's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of production. Mr. Bonetto¿s deposition testimony and plaintiffs¿ responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories preclude defendant from demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Bonetto was exposed to asbestos-containing products attributable to defendant. Even if Mr. Peckham¿s declaration is considered, it merely creates a triable issue when read in conjunction with Mr. Bonetto¿s deposition testimony. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: OSCAR BONETTO, ET AL VS. DOWMAN PRODUCTS, INC.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 12, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Thompson Building Materials, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden of production. Mr. Bonetto¿s deposition testimony and declaration and the deposition testimony of Howard Schutte taken in this case create a triable issue whether Mr. Bonetto was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Even if Mr. Thompson¿s declaration is considered, it merely creates a triable issue when read in conjunction with Mr. Bonetto¿s deposition testimony and declaration. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Bonetto¿s declaration are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: OSCAR BONETTO, ET AL VS. DOWMAN PRODUCTS, INC.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 12, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Mission Stucco Company¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issue 1 (negligence and strict liability) and granted as to issue 2 (punitive damages). Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of production. Mr. Bonetto¿s deposition testimony and plaintiffs¿ responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories preclude defendant from demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Bonetto was exposed to asbestos-containing products attributable to defendant. Even if Mr. Hernandez¿s declaration is considered, it merely creates a triable issue when read in conjunction with Mr. Bonetto¿s deposition testimony and declaration, defendant¿s responses to General Order interrogatories and Mr. Hernandez¿s deposition testimony. Issue 2 is granted because plaintiffs do not dispute that they are not asserting a claim for punitive damages against defendant. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
